title: Account of the Company of St. George in Norwich
summary: The checked-in XML is a repository copy of University of Michigan CME00122, encoded from printed p. 315 through the top portion of p. 334 of Benjamin Mackerell's 'Account of the Company of St. George in Norwich' in Norfolk Archaeology 3 (1852), pp. 315-374. The article resumes lower on p. 334 after the encoded endpoint. Its Mackerell-derived passages excerpted his 1737 manuscript History of Norwich, now described by Norfolk Record Office as NNAS S2/1/1-2; the anonymous society title and framing headnote are editorial additions. Mackerell states that he copied the first charter from the company's Book I marked A and a later patent from a company book; the present NRO record NCR 17b/9 contains the matching charter/ordinance and agreement material. Modern scholarship identifies Yelverton's Mediation specifically at NCR 17b/9, f. 189. A complete 1,090-page Internet Archive PDF and all canonical full-volume OCR, metadata, page-map, and scandata derivatives are cached with fixity and exact selection mapping; the complete Durkee thesis supporting the f. 189 identification is cached separately. No legitimate public facsimile of either physical NRO manuscript was found.
review_notes: The primary subject is the repository artifact CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00122.xml. Mackerell's manuscript, the medieval guild book, the 1852 article, Internet Archive facsimile, U-M encoding, ADS catalog description, and modern thesis are separate entities.

CME00122 encodes the article heading through the end of Yelverton's Mediation at the top of printed p. 334. The immediate article resumes lower on p. 334 and continues through p. 374; that continuation is omitted.

The complete source-volume mapping is printed p. 315 = Internet Archive PDF p. 773 = BookReader n775 and the encoded top portion of printed p. 334 = PDF p. 811 = n813. The full article continues through printed p. 374 = n893.

Mackerell's 'Book I, marked A' is strongly but not conclusively crosswalked to current NCR 17b/9. The exact f. 189 locus is independently supported only for Yelverton's Mediation; the first ordinances and patent retain unresolved exact loci.
The first represented text says the fraternity began in 1324, while the current NRO historical account says 1385. The normalized historical date is not inferred from the electronic text alone.

The 1852 edition prints 1451 for Yelverton's Mediation, while the current NRO catalog and Durkee date it 27 March 1452. The modern catalog date controls the normalized metadata while the edition reading remains recorded.
The inspected bound volume title page and Internet Archive metadata say 1852. U-M's publication field says 1851 and ADS labels the issue 1849; those provider conflicts remain explicit.

The NRO catalog records expose no digital objects. Exact-shelfmark searches found no public manuscript facsimile; the dated negative does not establish that NRO lacks an internal or request-deliverable surrogate.

The repository root LICENSE is the software-worded Unlicense. It contains no XML- or data-specific scope statement, so applicability to CME00122 markup/data is recorded as unknown rather than broadened into a confirmed public-domain dedication.

entities.label: Benjamin Mackerell, Account of the Company of St. George in Norwich (1852)
alternate_labels: Account of the Company of St. George in Norwich, from Mackerell's History of Norwich, MS. 1737
entities.description: The anonymously framed society publication of Mackerell's antiquarian account. The complete article occupies pp. 315-374; CME00122 encodes p. 315 through Yelverton's Mediation at the top of p. 334, not the continuation lower on that final page.

date_statements.label: Publication
date_statements.notes: The inspected volume title page controls this entity date; U-M says 1851 and ADS says 1849.

bibliographic.citation: Benjamin Mackerell, 'Account of the Company of St. George in Norwich,' from his manuscript History of Norwich (1737), Norfolk Archaeology, vol. 3 (Norwich: Norfolk and Norwich Archaeological Society, 1852), 315-374.
bibliographic.title: Account of the Company of St. George in Norwich
bibliographic.container_title: Norfolk Archaeology: or Miscellaneous Tracts Relating to the Antiquities of the County of Norfolk
bibliographic.publisher: Norfolk and Norwich Archaeological Society; printed by Charles Muskett
bibliographic.pages: 315-374; CME00122 encodes p. 315 through the top portion of p. 334

notes: The inspected article has no personal editor or byline. Its heading and society headnote attribute the account to Mackerell's 1737 manuscript in Hudson Gurney's possession.
The complete article continues after the encoded Yelverton text with Mackerell's commentary, further ordinances, office lists, pageantry, property, and dissolution history.

entities.label: Internet Archive digital facsimile of Norfolk Archaeology volume 3 (1852)
entities.description: Complete scan of the 1852 carrier volume containing Mackerell's article.
entities.reproduction_format: 1,090-page text PDF, OCR text, OCR XML, page-number map, scandata, and item metadata

entities.label: Norfolk Record Office, NNAS S2/1/1-2, Mackerell's manuscript History of Norwich
entities.description: Benjamin Mackerell's two-volume antiquarian manuscript, the immediate manuscript source named by the 1852 article.

date_statements.label: Compilation date
date_statements.value: 1737 according to the 1852 article; current catalog nd [pre 1738]

holding.notes: The separate MS 76-77 record says 'See NNAS S2/1/1-2'; whether it is a strict former shelfmark for the same physical set or a related catalog description should be confirmed by NRO.

notes: The current catalog says volume I has spine wear and should be used with care.
Volume I contains provenance notes, including purchase at the Strawberry Hill sale, and letters to Hudson Gurney dated 1852-1899.
The exact volume, folio, or page locus of the St George account is not stated in the public record.

entities.label: Norfolk Record Office, NCR 17b/9, St George's Company guild book
alternate_labels: Register of Guild members, assembly minutes and other memoranda
entities.description: The surviving guild volume whose current catalog contents match the charter/ordinance and 1452 agreement material transcribed by Mackerell; the Book I/A crosswalk is strongly supported but not explicitly stated by the current catalog.

date_statements.label: Current catalog date
date_statements.notes: The aggregate volume date is distinct from the historical dates of copied charters and agreements within it.

holding.notes: Mackerell's Book I/A designation is recorded as a proposed historical crosswalk, not an explicit current-catalog equivalence.

physical_description.support: Mainly paper with some parchment folios
physical_description.extent: 1 volume; Yelverton's Mediation at f. 189 according to Durkee
physical_description.format: Calf-leather binding on exposed original oak boards
notes: The calf is extensively torn and missing, and spine cords are exposed, but the NRO catalog says the text block is tight.

The catalog describes a rubricated calendar, inventories, charter-establishment statement and ordinances, member lists, a copy agreement dated 27 March 1452, assembly minutes, officer lists, and a member's oath.
Catalog languages are English and Latin; CME00122 represents English and Middle English documentary wording only.

entities.label: Dana Ann Durkee, Social Mobility and the Worsted Weavers of Norwich, c.1450-1530 (2017)
entities.description: A modern scholarly source used to verify the current archival locus and date of Yelverton's Mediation. The schema's catalog_description type is used provisionally for a scholarly aid rather than a source edition.

bibliographic.citation: Dana Ann Durkee, Social Mobility and the Worsted Weavers of Norwich, c.1450-1530 (doctoral thesis, Durham University, 2017).
bibliographic.title: Social Mobility and the Worsted Weavers of Norwich, c.1450-1530

notes: ADS gives volume 3 (1), article pp. 315-374, and issue year 1849, and links Internet Archive item norfolkarchaeol07socigoog. The direct volume-3 title page in the cached source says 1852.

locators: printed p. 315 through the top portion of p. 334, ending after Yelverton's Mediation

subject.description: Mackerell-derived account passages across article pp. 315-374, excluding the anonymous society-supplied title and framing headnote

object.description: Mackerell's St George account in the two-volume History of Norwich; exact volume and locus unresolved

scope.notes: The society-supplied article heading and headnote explicitly identify Mackerell's manuscript as the source of the account that follows, but those framing elements are not themselves modeled as excerpts from Mackerell. The public catalog does not expose the account's locus and no diplomatic comparison was possible.

subject.description: First charter and ordinances represented on printed pp. 316-325

object.description: Charter-establishment statement and ordinances in the guild book; exact folios unresolved

scope.notes: Mackerell explicitly says he copied this material from the company's Book I marked A. Current NCR 17b/9 has matching contents, but the historical-designation crosswalk and exact locus await NRO confirmation.

subject.description: English rendering of Henry V's patent represented on printed pp. 325-327

object.description: Possible corresponding charter material in the guild book; exact source and locus unresolved

scope.notes: Mackerell says only that he later transcribed it from a book belonging to the company. The current NCR 17b/9 catalog mentions a charter-establishment statement, but does not prove that this exact patent text or rendering is present.

subject.description: Yelverton's Mediation represented from printed p. 328 through the top of p. 334

locators: f. 189 according to Durkee 2017, p. 179 n. 44

scope.notes: The present archival locus and document identity are independently supported; exact textual fidelity still requires manuscript comparison.
The page range is confirmed; the ADS issue year 1849 conflicts with the inspected title-page date 1852.

access.access_method: Pre-book the NRO searchroom and request NNAS S2/1/1-2; production remains subject to condition and staff confirmation
notes: The catalog flags volume-I spine wear and asks that it be used with care.

access.access_method: NRO online document-order form using reference NNAS S2/1/1-2, or written enquiry for a whole-manuscript estimate and terms
access.format: Normally JPEG digital images; whole-object feasibility and specification by estimate
notes: The online service price is GBP 19.98 for up to six consecutive or sample images from one document; NRO gives an estimate for the rest and may charge GBP 60 per hour when more cost-effective. A complete two-volume facsimile may therefore be substantially more expensive and remains subject to condition.

For each of the two Mackerell volumes, specify a complete color object capture: front and back covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves or endleaves, blank leaves, every manuscript leaf recto and verso, and edges if offered. Ask NRO to preserve a stable image sequence and any foliation or pagination in the delivery.
Request publication permission and delivery terms separately; NRO asks that publication of facsimiles, transcripts, or extensive quotations be requested in writing to the County Archivist.

access.access_method: Exact-shelfmark catalog, web, repository, and facsimile search
Pre-book the NRO searchroom and request NCR 17b/9; production remains subject to condition and staff confirmation
NRO online document-order form using reference NCR 17b/9, or written enquiry for a whole-manuscript estimate and terms
access.format: Normally JPEG digital images; whole-object feasibility and specification by estimate
notes: The online service price is GBP 19.98 for up to six consecutive or sample images from one document; NRO gives an estimate for the rest and may charge GBP 60 per hour when more cost-effective.

For NCR 17b/9, specify a complete color object capture: front and back covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves or endleaves, blank leaves, every manuscript leaf recto and verso, and edges if offered. Ask NRO to preserve a stable image sequence and any foliation or pagination in the delivery.
Map the CME-related portions separately from that whole-object request: ask NRO to confirm the loci of the first charter and 1417 patent and to map Yelverton's Mediation at f. 189.

rights.component: Modern repository XML structure and markup; underlying historical and editorial text excluded
rights.jurisdiction: Repository license assertion of unclear data scope
rights.basis: The pinned CME root LICENSE is the Unlicense, but it expressly speaks of software, source, and binary forms and supplies no path-specific XML or data scope statement. This review records the assertion without treating CME00122 markup/data as conclusively dedicated to the public domain.
notes: Clarification from the repository maintainer would be needed before broad reuse claims for the markup/data layer.

rights.component: U-M electronic text and represented public-domain source material
rights.basis: The U-M item says the materials are in the public domain and applies No Copyright - United States. This is a rights statement, not an affirmative open license; provider service terms remain separate.

rights.component: Mackerell's pre-1738 manuscript wording and compilation; physical object, modern catalog text, and later images excluded
rights.basis: Mackerell died in 1738, his manuscript predates modern copyright by centuries, and the account was published in 1852. Physical custody and image-reproduction terms remain separate.

rights.component: Underlying medieval charter, ordinances, patent wording, and agreement embodied in the guild book; physical ownership, catalog text, and modern images excluded
rights.basis: The relevant wording and surviving volume date to the fifteenth and early sixteenth centuries and predate modern copyright by centuries. NRO custody and reproduction conditions are separate.

rights.component: The 1852 article, society framing, edition, and typography; later scan excluded
rights.basis: The article was published in 1852 and is in the U.S. public domain because works published before 1931 are outside the current U.S. copyright term in 2026.

rights.component: Internet Archive PDF scan, OCR, page maps, scandata, and item metadata representation
rights.basis: The exact item metadata contains no rights, licenseurl, or possible-copyright-status value. The age of the underlying volume establishes its U.S. public-domain status but does not itself create an affirmative license for every provider-created derivative or metadata field.

rights.component: Any present or future NRO-supplied digital images of NNAS S2/1/1-2; underlying Mackerell text excluded
rights.basis: No item-level image license is publicly attached because no public facsimile was found. NRO says requests to publish facsimiles, transcripts, or extensive quotations must be made in writing to the County Archivist, and its fees page distinguishes duplication from reproduction fees.
notes: The policy says no reproduction fee is charged for print runs of 2,000 or fewer, while website or e-book use is listed at GBP 120; written permission and any delivery-specific terms should still be preserved.

rights.component: Any present or future NRO-supplied digital images of NCR 17b/9; underlying medieval wording excluded
rights.basis: No item-level image license is publicly attached because no public facsimile was found. NRO says requests to publish facsimiles, transcripts, or extensive quotations must be made in writing to the County Archivist, and its fees page distinguishes duplication from reproduction fees.
The Durham e-Theses record links Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 3.0 for the deposited PDF.

editorial_practices.source_feature: The complete Mackerell article on printed pp. 315-374
editorial_practices.representation: Article heading on p. 315 through Yelverton's Mediation at the top of p. 334
editorial_practices.description: The electronic text encodes the article's opening selection and stops after 'according to the tenour of this agreement.' at the top of printed p. 334. Mackerell's continuation begins lower on that same page and runs through p. 374. The XML's 21 PB elements preserve one unnumbered opening marker plus printed page references 315-334.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Nineteenth-century framing, Mackerell's early modern prose, and embedded Middle English documents
editorial_practices.representation: One TEXT LANG='enm', one account DIV1, two HEADNOTE paragraphs, and three DIV2 units
editorial_practices.description: The item-level enm label does not distinguish its later English framing and patent rendering from the Middle English charter and mediation. The manifest therefore scopes languages and content parts independently of the single XML language wrapper.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Mackerell's wider two-volume History of Norwich
editorial_practices.representation: A sixty-page article introduced as locally interesting and sourced from the manuscript in Hudson Gurney's possession
editorial_practices.description: The society explains why it inserted Mackerell's account but names no personal editor and states no diplomatic method. The article must therefore be treated as an edited excerpt, not a facsimile or verified diplomatic transcript of NNAS S2/1/1-2.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Company books containing charters, ordinances, and agreements
editorial_practices.representation: Selected documentary transcriptions embedded in an antiquarian narrative
editorial_practices.description: Mackerell explicitly says he copied the first charter from Book I marked A and later found and transcribed another patent from a company book. He supplies no inspected statement of normalization, translation, or punctuation policy, so the printed wording is not assumed to be diplomatic and the English patent translator remains unknown.
editorial_practices.scope: Represented documents from article p. 316 through the top of p. 334

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00122.xml.
evidence.locator: TEMPHEAD, IDG, BODY/DIV1, two HEADNOTE elements, three DIV2 elements, and all descendants
evidence.summary: Direct evidence for the repository artifact, identifiers, encoded page selection, content sequence, 71 paragraph elements, later-English and Middle-English layers, superscript and italic representation, and the ending after Yelverton's Mediation at the top of printed p. 334.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME, root LICENSE at pinned commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.summary: The root Unlicense repeatedly describes software and source/binary forms but contains no path-specific XML or data scope statement; it supports recording the repository assertion and ambiguity, not a broadened markup/data conclusion.
notes: No source-cache duplicate was made because this is an immutable file in the same pinned git repository as the tracked XML.

evidence.citation: Parent-aware structural and source-boundary audit of the exact CME00122 XML.
evidence.summary: Confirmed 40,267 bytes and git blob d148227be1810385e2a1b37ea16ca254171cb4e1; one DIV1, three DIV2, four HEAD, two HEADNOTE, 71 P, 21 PB, two CLOSER, one DATELINE, two DATE, one SIGNED, one TAILNOTE, four HI, 65 SUP, and no GAP, UNCLEAR, NOTE, LG, or L. The article scan confirms that the XML stops at the top of p. 334; the unencoded continuation starts lower on the same printed page.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Account of the Company of St. George, in Norwich, CME00122 item page.
evidence.summary: Official item evidence for stable identifier, expanded title, Mackerell attribution, source volume, subject, public contents, and item-level Public Domain / No Copyright-United States statement. Its publication field says 1851, conflicting with the source title page.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse collection information and copyright statement.
evidence.summary: Collection-level evidence for keyed public-domain editions, in-house proofing and tagging, electronic-text context, public access, and No Copyright-United States framing.
notes: This mutable policy page is retained by canonical URL and access date; the exact repository XML and fixed source editions are tracked or cached separately.

evidence.summary: Primary evidence for the 1852 imprint, article identity and complete boundary, anonymous society headnote, Mackerell's manuscript and company-book source statements, represented documents and dates, exact electronic selection, and omitted continuation.

It identifies English and Latin, paper and parchment, and no public digital object.

evidence.citation: Frank Meeres, 'Outliving St George,' Norfolk Record Office blog, 23 April 2026.
evidence.summary: Current archive historical account dating the guild's foundation to 1385 and royal charter to 1417, locating its surviving records in NCR cases 8e-g and 17b, and describing relief, procession, Reformation survival, and transformation into the Company of St George.

evidence.summary: Modern scholarly cross-check dating the reform to 1452, identifying Yelverton's Mediation as NRO NCR 17b/9, f. 189, and citing Mackerell's 1852 article at pp. 315-374.

evidence.citation: Dated exact-shelfmark public-facsimile search for NRO NNAS S2/1/1-2, MS 76-77, and NCR 17b/9.
evidence.summary: The official records expose no digital objects, access copies, or facsimile links, and exact-shelfmark searches across general web indexes and public repositories found no legitimate manuscript images. The 1852 printed article and modern thesis are not manuscript facsimiles.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, Duration of Copyright FAQ.

open_questions.question: Where exactly in NNAS S2/1/1-2 is Mackerell's St George account, and can NRO confirm that Book I marked A is current NCR 17b/9 and supply exact loci for the first ordinances and 1417 patent?
next_steps: Request a volume/folio crosswalk for the Mackerell account from NRO.
Ask NRO to confirm the Book I/A-to-NCR 17b/9 equivalence and identify each represented document's exact locus.
Keep Durkee's NCR 17b/9 f. 189 identification scoped only to Yelverton's Mediation until other loci are independently verified.

open_questions.question: Can complete, foliation-stable color facsimiles of NNAS S2/1/1-2 and NCR 17b/9 be obtained and mapped to CME00122?
next_steps: Ask NRO whether complete existing digital surrogates already exist before commissioning new photography.
Where condition and cost permit, request the complete two-volume Mackerell manuscript and complete NCR 17b/9 rather than cropped work excerpts.
Preserve the full delivery, inventory, checksum, citation, permission, and pricing terms, then map the work portions inside the unchanged complete objects.
Do not infer image completeness from a small online sample or from the 1852 article.
notes: The order route is actionable, but whole-object feasibility and cost require an NRO estimate and condition review.

open_questions.question: What documentary or calendrical history explains the 1324/1385 foundation conflict and the edition's 1451 versus current-catalog 1452 date for Yelverton's Mediation?
next_steps: Inspect NCR 17b/9 and the 1389 guild return directly, including numeral forms and later additions.
Compare a modern critical edition of the guild records with Mackerell's manuscript and the 1852 print.
Do not silently normalize source readings without preserving each layer and its evidence.

open_questions.question: Do original issue wrappers or society records establish an earlier installment date that explains U-M's 1851 and ADS's 1849 values despite the bound-volume title page dated 1852?
next_steps: Inspect original issue wrappers and society proceedings rather than inferring issue dates from later catalog metadata.
Use 1852 for this inspected volume entity until distinct issue-level evidence is found.
Consider reporting the conflict to U-M and ADS.
